name: Holyoke Community
description_short: Imperial Credit Union is a member-owned, NCUA-insured credit union serving members and families of the African Methodist Episcopal Church in Illinois and parts of Iowa.
description_long:
Imperial Credit Union was founded to serve members and families of the African Methodist Episcopal (AME) Church of the Illinois Conference Branch, which includes parts of Iowa. The organization operates as a volunteer-based credit union dedicated to providing financial services to those underserved or unserved by traditional institutions. As of the available information, Imperial Credit Union is the oldest continuously African American-owned business based in Springfield, Illinois, reflecting its deep community roots and long-standing commitment to its membership.

The credit union offers a range of basic financial services including savings accounts with NCUA insurance protection up to $250,000, share-secured loans, and personal installment loans. Members can access funds through multiple channels including phone, email, and PayPal withdrawal requests. The credit union specifically advertises a 10/10 Loan program ($1,000 at 10% interest with up to 12 months to repay) and share-secured loans at 5% interest for members with balances over $500. The organization also sells gift cards in denominations from $10 to $1,000, including virtual cards for online shopping.

What distinguishes Imperial Credit Union is its niche membership model tied to the AME Church community and its emphasis on serving financially underserved populations without traditional credit checks on share-secured loans. The credit union operates as a volunteer organization with a board of community leaders and maintains a personal, accessible service model where members can withdraw funds by phone call or email request. Their share-secured loan product specifically avoids credit score inquiries, which appeals to those with damaged credit histories.

However, Imperial Credit Union operates with significant limitations. The organization appears to be very small with only two paid staff members (interim manager and assistant manager/loan officer), limiting service capacity. Loan products are modest in scope ($500-$2,500 for share-secured loans), and the 10% interest rate on their signature loan program is notably higher than many mainstream credit unions. The volunteer structure and limited online presence suggest minimal digital banking capabilities. Membership is restricted to AME Church members and families, significantly limiting who can join.
services: Savings accounts with NCUA insurance (up to $250,000)
10/10 Loan program ($1,000 at 10% APR, up to 12 months to repay)
Digital/virtual gift cards for online shopping
pros: NCUA insurance protects deposits up to $250,000, same as major banks
Share-secured loans available at 5% interest with no credit check required
Accessible withdrawal methods including phone, email, and PayPal requests
Oldest continuously African American-owned business in Springfield, Illinois with deep community roots
Share-secured loan balances remain in savings account after loan payoff
No credit score threat from share-secured loans even if delinquent (auto-withdrawal from account)
10/10 Loan program offers $1,000 at 10% APR with flexible 12-month repayment
cons: Membership restricted to African Methodist Episcopal Church members and families, limiting eligibility
Very small organization with only two full-time staff members, limiting service capacity and hours
10% interest rate on signature loans is high compared to mainstream credit unions
Limited digital banking infrastructure; relies on phone, email, and manual processes
Loan products capped at modest amounts ($500-$2,500 for share-secured loans)
best_for: AME Church members and families seeking basic savings and small installment loans
Individuals with poor credit histories who want to access credit without credit checks
Community members looking to support an African American-owned financial institution
diagnosis: Imperial Credit Union is best for AME Church members seeking basic savings accounts and small loans without credit checks. The main caveat is that membership is restricted to the AME Church community, and the organization's small size and volunteer structure limit service capacity and product sophistication.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Holyoke Community — Credit Union in Holyoke, CO | CreditDoc
meta_description: Holyoke Community — NCUA-insured credit union in Holyoke, CO. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
